summarization.a: The scatter plot shows the relationship between injection level and effective lifetime for two different thickness passivation layers (30 nm and 15 nm). The data points are clustered around a curve, indicating a non-linear relationship.

data_extraction.a:
| Injection level Δn (cm⁻³) | Effective lifetime (s), 30 nm|Effective lifetime (s), 15 nm|
|---|---|---|
| 10¹² | 2*10⁻⁴ |-|
| 10¹³ | 4*10⁻⁴ |10⁻³|
| 10¹⁴ | 6*10⁻⁴ |10⁻³ |
| 10¹⁵ | 9*10⁻⁴ |10⁻³ |
| 10¹⁶ | 10⁻³ |10⁻³ |
| 10¹⁷ | 10⁻⁴ |10⁻⁴ |

a.question: Which thickness of passivation seems to passivate the silicon the best?
a.answer: a	The 15 nm thick layer, this thickness seems to have the best effect over a broader range of injection level.

a.question: What is the peak effective lifetime with a passivation of 30 nm aluminum oxide?
What are signs in the graph that there is no linear relation between the injection level and the effective lifetime?
a.answer: a	Scattered data does not show a straight line. In addition, both axes are logarithmic.
